evidence.page_title: Obama brags about his Asian cooking skills - Geo News
evidence.title: Obama brags about his Asian cooking skills
evidence.description: Former US president Barack Obama may go down as one of the most influential leaders in world history, but the real 'Obama factor' is how typically un-presidential he can be at times. Talking to veteran Indian...
evidence.text:
Published December 01, 2017
Former US president Barack Obama may go down as one of the most influential leaders in world history, but the real 'Obama factor' is how typically un-presidential he can be at times.
Talking to veteran Indian journalist Karan Thapar at the Hindustan Times Leadership Summit 2017 in New Delhi on Friday, the two-term US president boasted about his cooking skills.
“I’m pretty sure I’m the first US president to have the dal recipe. My keema is also excellent... my chicken is okay,” he quipped.
When asked if he knew how to make roti, Obama said, “Ah, no, making a chapati is too hard”.
Earlier, in 2013, when then Pakistani premier Nawaz Sharif met Obama at the White House, the US president told Sharif he knows how to cook daal and qeema.
Recalling his visit to Pakistan during his college days in the 1980s, Obama said he was taught how to cook the dishes by the mother of his Pakistani roommates.
"I shared with him (Sharif) that I had the opportunity, back in 1980 when I was a very young man, to visit Pakistan because I had two Pakistani roommates in college whose mothers taught me how to cook daal and qeema, and other very good Pakistani food," Obama told reporters during their meeting.
"And it was a wonderful trip for me, and created a great appreciation and a great love for the Pakistani people," Obama said in his joint media appearance with Sharif.
Concluding their meeting, Sharif had invited the Obamas to Pakistan, saying "Qeema and daal are waiting".

evidence.page_title: Obama makes burritos, Biden serves lunches on MLK Day of Service
evidence.title: Administration participate in community service on MLK day - UPI.com
evidence.description: President Obama prepared burritos and Vice President Joe Biden dished out lunch in the Washington area Monday in commemoration of Martin Luther King Jr. Day.
evidence.text:
WASHINGTON, Jan. 20 (UPI) -- President Obama prepared burritos and Vice President Joe Biden dished out lunch in the Washington area Monday in commemoration of Martin Luther King Jr. Day.
To commemorate Martin Luther King Jr. Day of Service, the first family participated in a service project at the D.C. Central Kitchen, a community kitchen, preparing meals for distribution to local shelters.
Obama, first lady Michelle Obama and daughter Malia and Sasha worked the line, preparing burritos along with other volunteers.
"I just want to thank everybody here for the great work they do," Obama said, noting it was the kitchen's 25th anniversary.
Obama also tracked inventory -- shouting down the line that burritos were running low. Answered what was inside the burrito, he said it was either lamb or beef, then settling on beef.
Biden joined members of the Catholic Volunteer Network, serving hot lunches to guests at So Others Might Eat in Washington.
He also spoke at the National Action Network's Annual Martin Luther King Jr. Day Breakfast.
"It's the everyday actions that inspire that are going to keep human progress rolling forward and keep it from sliding back," Biden said.
Members of Obama's Cabinet also participated in the Day of Service, including Treasury Secretary Jack Lew sorting and packing clothing for homeless veterans and Commerce Secretary Penny Pritzker serving food at Food and Friends, a Washington organization that prepares and delivers meals and groceries to people living with HIV/AIDS, cancer and other catastrophic illnesses.

evidence.page_title: La familia Obama prepara comida para repartir entre los más ...
evidence.title: La familia Obama prepara comida para repartir entre los más necesitados
evidence.text:
MADRID, 21 Ene. (CHANCE) -
La familia Obama se ha puesto el delantal y ha pasado una mañana ayudando a preparar comida para repartir entre los más necesitados. Cada año se celebra en Estados Unidos el día de Martín Luther King el tercer lunes de enero y es tradición dedicar esta jornada a hacer labores de voluntariado.
El presidente Obama es conocido por su actitud muy participativa en la vida pública estadounidense, en la que hace partícipes a sus dos hijas pequeñas Malia y Shasa, recientemente nombradas iconos de moda por varias revistas americanas.
Michelle también acompaña a su marido e hijas en esta tarea tan solidaria. Recientemente la primera dama cumplía 50 años rodeada de su familia y amigos en la Casa Blanca con la participación de la mismísima Beyoncé en la fiesta.
Una acción muy solidaria que incita a todo el pueblo americano a tomar ejemplo de su presidente y ayudar a los más necesitados. Obama, muy activo en su cuenta de Twitter ha querido también rendir un homenaje más al líder Martín Luther King, publicando una de sus frases más célebres: "La oscuridad no puede quitar la oscuridad; solo la luz puede. El odio no puede quitar el odio; solo el amor puede"
